ogTitle: South Trayning vs South Yilgarn: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Trayning and South Yilgarn.
heading: South Trayning vs South Yilgarn — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Trayning, Western Australia and South Yilgarn, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Trayning has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to South Yilgarn (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Trayning or South Yilgarn safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Trayning has a flood risk score of 28/100, while South Yilgarn scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Trayning scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. South Yilgarn scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Trayning or South Yilgarn?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Trayning has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
